video_title: Cobra Kai: Season 6 Part 3 Sneak Peek | Netflix
reply_text: Kreese was suffering even before he went to Vietnam. His mother killed herself and he was an outcast as a result. Being many decades before the show began, the blooming stigma surrounding mental health prevented him from properly dealing with his issues, and his treatment of Johnny and getting his ass whooped by Miyagi in retaliation for trying to murder Johnny, all of these things, which he didn't address, pushed him off the slippery slope. Sadly though, because of his current actions, his trauma and past suffering is no longer an excuse for everything he has done. While he might have been in the wrong for rearing Tory and Johnny, he still had good intentions because he wanted to be the parental figure in their lives they never had, and while Johnny may have realized what Kreese has done to him, Tory hasn't, simply because she was trying to get over her mother's unexpected death. Kreese knew how to deal with her, but so did Johnny, because of how their mothers died. While Johnny wanted her to keep fighting, the Larussos didn't want their daughter to get hurt, which is why they stopped her from fairly winning, but Tory believed it was nepotism, and decided to side with Kreese. Hopefully, she will be able to make peace with everyone at Miyagi Do, and they'll forgive her. Whether or not Kreese or Silver will get their redemption arc is debatable, because of their actions, as while Kreese is taking responsibility, Silver is essentially a demon because of having tortured Daniel and his friends. Kim though, I really do hope she gets a redemption arc, better than her American friends, since Kwon's death likely made her realize that making her life all about karate, spending it under her granddad's thumb, was hardly even worth it, and that she can no longer hide her trauma from Tory, or deal with her granddad's wrath. Perhaps by the end of the series, Tory will hopefully forgive Kim for everything, but not either Kreese or Silver.

reply_text: Whether or not Kreesey will get his redemption arc is highly debatable. He's already on the run from the law for escaping prison, so it's very unlikely he'll be able to get any peace. Daniel and Johnny will probably struggle to forgive Kreese when the time comes, which will not sit well with him because of all their grudges. As for Silver, he was already even a million times worse than Kreese because of his actions, like cheating to win, corrupting his students, and regularly torturing Daniel and his allies both physically and psychologically. It won't be long before his breakdown becomes his downfall, and I look forward to seeing him die karmically. As for Kim, I at least believe that she deserves a better redemption arc for everything that happened. Remember how she gave up everything in her life just for karate, thinking it was well worth it, but was clearly lying to herself, more than to Tory, whom she would have to mend her relationship with, and Kwon's death proved that she was not going to be able to turn back. I hope that she can deal with her granddad and make peace with Tory, after all her suffering.
